summary: Block the hand-added --cookie-file flag from MCP override: drop it in cliArgsFromMCP and omit it from the mirrored tool schema.
reason: cookie-file is a Tier-1 credential/path flag hand-added to root.go for this keyless fork (see medium-reader-cookie-file-flag). The cobratree MCP mirror forwarded any non-denylisted root flag, so an MCP client (e.g. via prompt injection from article content) could set {"cookie-file": "/any/path"} and point the companion binary at an arbitrary file or an attacker-supplied session. The generator's denylist is correct for generated output (it never emits a credential flag), so this is a printed-CLI fix, not an upstream one: cli-printing-press issue #3183 was closed as not-a-bug.
validated_outcome: go test ./... and go vet ./... green with MEDIUM_API_KEY unset; a live tools/list against the built MCP server shows cookie-file is not a settable property on any of the 10 tools, while all legitimate per-command params remain.
